tools.name: ReplyToEmail
tools.description: Send a reply to an email message.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The body of the email

parameters.name: reply_to_message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the message to reply to

parameters.name: reply_to_whom
parameters.description: Whether to reply to every recipient (including cc) or only to the original sender. Defaults to 'GmailReplyToWhom.ONLY_THE_SENDER'.

parameters.name: cc
parameters.description: Additional CC recipients. When replying to every recipient, merged with CC from the original message; duplicates are removed (RFC-aware). Duplicates of the reply's To recipients are dropped from Cc (RFC-aware mailbox comparison). The authenticated user's own email address is removed from Cc on replies.

parameters.name: bcc
parameters.description: BCC recipients of the email. The authenticated user's own email address is removed from Bcc on replies.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: The content type of the email body. Defaults to 'plain'

output.description: A dictionary containing the sent email details with date in descriptive UTC format

body.value: Thank you for reaching out! I have reviewed your proposal and would be happy to schedule a follow-up call to discuss the details further. Please let me know your availability for next week.

tools.name: UpdateDraftEmail
tools.description:
Update an existing email draft using the Gmail API.

Single-part plain drafts support full body replacement. For HTML, attachments, or
multipart drafts, the tool fails unless the body is unchanged (metadata-only update),
in which case the existing MIME tree is preserved. Otherwise edit the draft in Gmail.

For each of subject, body, recipient, cc, and bcc, omitting the parameter or passing
``None`` leaves that part of the draft unchanged (for cc/bcc, existing headers are kept;
pass an empty list to clear).

parameters.name: draft_email_id
parameters.description: The ID of the draft email to update.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Draft subject. Omit or pass None to leave the existing subject unchanged.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Plain text for merge and rebuild. Omit or pass None to use the existing draft body (plain text, or single-part HTML decoded as text for comparison). Unsupported MIME (HTML with attachments, multipart) is rejected unless the effective body is unchanged so a headers-only update applies.

parameters.name: recipient
parameters.description: To header. Omit or pass None to leave the existing recipient unchanged.

parameters.name: cc
parameters.description: Cc recipients. Omit or pass None to preserve the existing Cc header; pass an empty list to clear Cc.

parameters.name: bcc
parameters.description: Bcc recipients. Omit or pass None to preserve the existing Bcc header; pass an empty list to clear Bcc.

output.description: A dictionary containing the updated draft email details with date in descriptive UTC format

subject.value: Updated Meeting Agenda for Q3 Planning

body.value:
Hi Team,

Please find the updated agenda for our Q3 planning meeting scheduled for Friday at 2 PM.

1. Review Q2 results
2. Set Q3 targets
3. Assign action items

Best regards,
Alex

This tool provides detailed information about the authenticated user including
their name, email, profile picture, Gmail account statistics, and other
important profile details from Google services.

parameters.name: reply_to_message_id
parameters.description: The Gmail message ID of the message to draft a reply to

parameters.name: reply_to_whom
parameters.description: Whether to reply to every recipient (including cc) or only to the original sender. Defaults to 'GmailReplyToWhom.ONLY_THE_SENDER'.

parameters.name: cc
parameters.description: Additional CC recipients. When replying to every recipient, merged with CC from the original message; duplicates are removed (RFC-aware). Duplicates of the reply's To recipients are dropped from Cc (RFC-aware mailbox comparison). The authenticated user's own email address is removed from Cc on replies.

parameters.name: bcc
parameters.description: Bcc recipients of the draft reply email. The authenticated user's own email address is removed from Bcc on replies.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: The content type of the email body. Defaults to 'plain'

output.description: Draft details; use draft_email_id (or id) for update/send/delete—not message.id.

body.value:
Hi John,

Thank you for your email. I have reviewed the document and everything looks good. Let's schedule a call for Thursday at 2 PM to discuss further.

Best regards,
Alice

documentationChunks.content:
The Arcade Gmail MCP Server uses the [Google auth provider](/references/auth-providers/google) to connect to users' Google accounts.
---

summary:
The Gmail toolkit provides Arcade LLM tools for interacting with Gmail via the Google OAuth2 API. It enables agents and applications to read, compose, send, organize, and manage email on behalf of authenticated users.

## Capabilities

- **Reading & searching:** List emails, threads, and drafts; search threads; retrieve threads by ID; look up emails by header field.
- **Sending & replying:** Send new emails, reply to messages, and send existing drafts directly.
- **Drafts management:** Create, update, and delete drafts; compose draft replies; supports metadata-only updates for HTML/multipart drafts (body replacement limited to plain single-part drafts).
- **Labels & organization:** List, create, and apply/remove labels; move emails to trash.
- **Account introspection:** Retrieve the authenticated user's profile, email address, display name, profile picture, and Gmail account statistics.

## OAuth

This toolkit uses OAuth 2.0 via the **Google** provider. See the [Arcade Google auth provider docs](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/references/auth-providers/google) for setup instructions, including how to configure the provider in your Arcade environment.
